payload.title: Audit Committee Meeting
payload.description: Audit Committee Book for the MTA Audit Committee meeting held on May 22, 2023, including meeting minutes, work plan, management review of consolidated financial statements, and draft 2022 audited financial statements for MTA and its component units.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Metropolitan Transportation Authority
Audit Committee Meeting

payload.claim_text: The 2020-2024 Capital Program totaling $54,799 million covering the years 2020-2024 for commuter railroad operations (LIRR, Metro-North), transit system (NYC Transit, MaBSTOA, MTA Bus Company, Staten Island Railway) and MTA Bridges and Tunnels was originally approved by the MTA Board on September 25, 2019.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: 2020-2024 Capital Program – Capital programs totaling $54,799 covering the years 2020-2024

payload.claim_text: The combined funding sources for the 2020-2024 MTA Capital Programs and the 2020-2024 MTA Bridges and Tunnels Capital Program include $15,000 million in Central Business District tolling sources, $10,000 million in new revenue sources, $8,041 million in MTA bonds and PAYGO, $3,327 million in MTA Bridges and Tunnels bonds, $12,424 million in Federal funds, $3,101 million in State of New York funding, $3,007 million in City of New York funding, and $542 million in other contributions.
payload.subject: MTA Capital Program funding

evidence_refs.source_quote: funding sources for the 2020–2024 MTA Capital Programs and the 2020-2024 MTA Bridges and Tunnels Capital Program, include $15,000 in Central Business District tolling sources

payload.claim_text: The Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) was authorized by the MTA Reform and Traffic Mobility Act and enacted in April 2019. It will impose a toll for vehicles entering or remaining in the Central Business District (CBD), defined as Manhattan south of and inclusive of 60th Street, not including the FDR Drive or the West Side Highway (which includes the Battery Park underpass and or any surface roadway portion of the Hugh L. Carey Tunnel that connects to West Street).
payload.subject: Central Business District Tolling Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Central Business District Tolling Program (CBDTP) was authorized by the MTA Reform and Traffic Mobility Act and enacted in April 2019

payload.claim_text: The MTA received total federal COVID relief funds of $15.1 billion since the start of the pandemic: $4.0 billion in 2020 from the CARES Act, $4.1 billion in 2021 from CRRSAA, and $7.0 billion in 2022 from the American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA).
